Pharma Salts India 2026

Interactive Clinical Index · Top 529 Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients  ·  Last Updated: 01 Apr 2026

🔍
☀️ 🌙

Complete ranked clinical analysis of 529 pharmaceutical salts dominating the Indian market in 2026. Each card shows brand names, drug schedule, section, dosing, pharmacokinetics, contraindications, and full clinical details — designed as a bedside companion for clinicians, pharmacists, and medical students.

Badge & Schedule Legend

OTC — Over-the-Counter C — Schedule C (by Rx) H — Schedule H (Rx Only) H1 — Schedule H1 (Restricted) X — Schedule X (Narcotics/Strict) NLEM — National List of Essential Medicines First-line — Preferred initial therapy Second-line — Used when first-line fails/intolerant

FDA Pregnancy Categories

A — Controlled studies show no risk B — No evidence of risk in humans C — Risk cannot be ruled out D — Positive evidence of risk X — Contraindicated in pregnancy

Filters & Search

Use the Safety Filters to exclude hepatotoxic or nephrotoxic drugs, or show only pediatric-approved salts. Combine pill filters for Section, Schedule, Category, Drug Class, or Pregnancy Category. The search bar scans across salt name, brand, uses, formation, category, side effects, drug interactions, clinical pearls, and avoid/contraindication fields for comprehensive results.

Abbreviations Glossary

OD — Once daily
BID — Twice daily
TID — Thrice daily
QID — Four times/day
PRN — As needed
HS — At bedtime
AC — Before meals
PC — After meals
eGFR — Estimated GFR
CrCl — Creatinine clearance
LFT — Liver function test
FBS — Fasting blood sugar
PPBS — Post-prandial BS
HbA1c — Glycated Hb
INR — Intl. Normalised Ratio
CBC — Complete blood count
T½ — Half-life
Tmax — Time to peak
CYP — Cytochrome P450
NLEM — Natl Essential Med.
CDSCO — Drug regulator India
NPPA — Price authority India
MRP — Max retail price
DDD — Defined daily dose
